title: ds-viz-fillergauge - option matrix (light)
description: Test bench for splunk.fillergauge. The 10.4 reference exposes 7 options: backgroundColor, gaugeColor, labelDisplay, majorTickInterval, orientation, value, valueDisplay. Same shape as splunk.markergauge BUT the bar fills with one solid colour (gaugeColor) instead of banded zones (gaugeRanges). DOS expressions on gaugeColor enable threshold-based fill colours.

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval value = round(60 + 32*sin(i/4) + random()%5) | table _time value

ds_health_swing.name: health score - swings across RAG buckets

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval value = round(60 + 35*sin(i/4) + random()%5) | table _time value

options.markdown:
# `splunk.fillergauge` - option matrix

A single value plotted as a *filled bar* against a 0-100 scale (default). Same shape and option set as `splunk.markergauge` but the entire bar fills with one solid colour (`gaugeColor`) instead of resolving to banded zones (`gaugeRanges`).

**Use it when** the message is *how full / how complete* (capacity used, quota progress, completion %) - the fill area itself reads as the answer. **Use `markergauge` instead when** the message is *which band are we in* (NPS zones, latency budget categories).

DOS expressions on `gaugeColor` enable threshold-based fills - the fill colour flips when the value crosses a threshold.

7 options total. **Default orientation is vertical.**

viz_default_low.title: 1. Default - vertical, low value (22)
viz_default_low.description: Pure default. orientation=vertical, gaugeColor falls back to theme primary (#7B49B7 on prisma dark). The bar fills 22% from the bottom.

viz_default_mid.title: 2. Default - vertical, mid value (58)
viz_default_mid.description: Same options, mid value. The fill reaches the middle.

viz_default_high.title: 3. Default - vertical, high value (94)
viz_default_high.description: Same options, high value. The bar is nearly full.

viz_horizontal.title: 4. orientation: horizontal
viz_horizontal.description: Same data, horizontal axis. Reads left-to-right; the fill grows from the left edge as the value increases. Useful in dense KPI rows.

viz_static_color.title: 5. gaugeColor static (#2E8B57)
viz_static_color.description: Hard-coded fill colour. Use when the dashboard already encodes severity elsewhere (in the title, in a sibling tile) and the gauge just needs to match.

viz_static_amber.title: 6. gaugeColor static (#E89A2C)
viz_static_amber.description: Same panel, amber. Reading both 5 and 6 side by side proves the gauge isn't using gaugeRanges - it's a single solid fill controlled by gaugeColor.

viz_dynamic_color.title: 7. gaugeColor dynamic via rangeValue
viz_dynamic_color.description: DOS expression flips the fill colour on threshold. Pattern: '> primary | seriesByName(field) | lastPoint() | rangeValue(thresholds)'. Disjoint, gap-free buckets - 'to' is exclusive (< X), 'from' is inclusive (>= X). Red < 50, amber 50-80, green >= 80. Data swings 25-95 so all three buckets demo on reload.

viz_disk_used.title: 8. Disk used (high=bad inverse thresholds)
viz_disk_used.description: Inverse semantics: high value is BAD. Same DOS pattern, thresholds flipped - green < 60, amber 60-85, red >= 85. Disjoint, gap-free buckets - 'to' is exclusive, 'from' is inclusive. Data swings 30-95 so all three buckets demo on reload. The visual fill area reads as 'space consumed'.

viz_quota.title: 9. Quota progress (max 1000)
viz_quota.description: fillergauge has NO maxValue option (unlike singlevalueradial). The scale is implicit 0-100 by default; for non-percentage values like 'orders out of 1000', map upstream in SPL: `eval value = round(orders / 1000 * 100)`. The label can clarify the absolute number.

viz_percentage_labels.title: 10. labelDisplay: percentage, valueDisplay: percentage
viz_percentage_labels.description: Render axis labels and the value readout as percentages. With a 0-100 scale these are identical to the numbers. The 'percentage' mode becomes meaningful only if you re-scale the gauge upstream (eg. when the underlying value is in a non-percentage unit).

viz_label_off.title: 11. labelDisplay: off, valueDisplay: off
viz_label_off.description: Strips axis labels and the value readout. The fill area alone carries the message. Useful when the dashboard already has the number in a sibling singlevalue tile.

viz_majortick_bg.title: 12. majorTickInterval + custom backgroundColor
viz_majortick_bg.description: majorTickInterval is in pixels, not values - controls major tick spacing along the axis. backgroundColor is set to a panel-tint hex for alarm tiles. gaugeColor and backgroundColor must contrast - dark fill on dark bg disappears.

options.markdown:
## Compact tile patterns

The Studio editor refuses to resize a `splunk.fillergauge` panel below ~200x150 (vertical) or ~200x100 (horizontal). The trick to get a denser KPI bank: **set `backgroundColor: "transparent"` and place panels at the minimum size, edge-to-edge.** The panel chrome disappears and only the bar artwork remains, so four bars side-by-side read as one tight unit. Below: a 4-up vertical bank (panels 13-16) and a stacked horizontal bank (panels 17-20). Compare with panels 1-3 which use the default opaque chrome.

**Rules:**
- Vertical: 200x150 minimum (lower than markergauge - the bar doesn't need band labels). Use `labelDisplay: "off"` + `valueDisplay: "off"` and let a sibling singlevalue carry the number.
- Horizontal: 200x100 minimum.
- `backgroundColor: "transparent"` is the unlock - without it the surface card swamps the bar.
- For dynamic colour use the same DOS pattern as panels 7-8 - threshold-driven `gaugeColor` works inside compact tiles.

viz_compact_v1.title: CPU
viz_compact_v1.description: Compact vertical tile - 200x150, transparent bg, value/label off. The fill height alone carries the message; sibling number tiles can carry the percentage.

viz_compact_v2.title: MEM
viz_compact_v2.description: Mid value - the bar is half-filled. Static gaugeColor here; production might use the dynamic-rangeValue pattern from panel 7.

viz_compact_v3.title: DISK
viz_compact_v3.description: High value - the bar is nearly full. Red gaugeColor signals trouble; the eye reads 'almost full + red' as one signal.

viz_compact_v4.title: NET
viz_compact_v4.description: Fourth tile. The four-up bank read together as a CPU/MEM/DISK/NET strip - dense, no panel chrome, fills do the talking.

viz_compact_h1.title: ingest queue
viz_compact_h1.description: Horizontal compact tile - 200x100, transparent bg. The fill grows left-to-right.

viz_compact_h2.title: search queue
viz_compact_h2.description: Stacked under panel 17 with an 8 px gap. The vertical rhythm groups them as a queue panel.

viz_compact_h3.title: indexer queue
viz_compact_h3.description: Third row - high value, red. The colour ramp green-amber-red carries severity.

viz_compact_h4.title: fwd queue
viz_compact_h4.description: Bottom row. Together panels 17-20 fit in 220x424 - more vertical than the markergauge equivalent because fillergauge horizontal min is the same 100h.
